rxn_class_summary: Electroreductive dual-radical tri-/difluoromethylarylation of alkenes coupling cyanoarenes with (di/tri)fluoromethylsulfones using a sacrificial Zn anode and a naphthalene-type mediator (Med-1)
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction generates two radicals: cyanoarene (2) is reduced to its radical anion (I), which then mediates SET to the fluoroalkylsulfone (3aa) to release CF3 radical; in parallel, Med-1 is cathodically reduced to its radical anion which provides an alternative SET pathway to 3aa. Zn sacrificial anode supplies electrons by oxidation.

key_intermediates.name: benzyl radical adduct from CF3• + alkene (III)
cyanide anion released upon radical/radical-anion coupling

electron_flow: At the cathode, cyanoarene 2 (and/or Med-1) is reduced by one electron to a persistent radical anion, which performs SET to the fluoroalkylsulfone 3aa to fragment it into ArSO2- and CF3•; CF3• adds to alkene 1 forming a benzyl radical (III) that undergoes radical/radical-anion cross-coupling with the cyanoarene radical anion (I), expelling CN- to give the arylated product. Electrons originate from oxidation of the sacrificial Zn anode.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Products 4aa and 7aa were not obtained in its presence, consistent with radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: HRMS detection of CF2H adduct
probe_reagents.observation: Difluoromethyl radical captured and detected by HRMS; aryl radical and CF3 radical not observed directly

probe_reagents.name: radical clock substrate
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened products 10 and 11 formed in 56% and 43% yield, supporting a dual-radical mechanism

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1aa/2aa/3aa/Med-1
probe_reagents.observation: 1aa not reduced in window; 2aa reversible Ered = -1.86 V; 3aa irreversible Ered = -2.05 V; Med-1 reversible Ered = -2.86 V; addition of 3aa enhances catalytic current of 2aa and suppresses oxidation, indicating SET from 2aa•- to 3aa; 3aa/Med-1 mixture shows decreased oxidation and increased catalytic current of Med-1, confirming SET; 2aa charge consumption 6.9 F/mol indicates multi-step SET chain

notes: Two cathodic SET pathways operate in parallel: (a) direct reduction of cyanoarene 2 to its radical anion, which reduces sulfone 3aa to CF3•; (b) Med-1-mediated pathway in which the naphthalene mediator radical anion reduces 3aa. Multi-step SET chain mechanism is invoked given charge consumption far exceeds 1 F/mol. Final C–C bond formation is proposed as radical/radical-anion cross-coupling with extrusion of CN-, rather than radical addition followed by rearomatization.
